summary: Tested GPS/Map functionality fixes for Samsung S25 FE user experiencing 'Invalid LatLng object: (NaN, NaN)' errors. All fixes verified working: isValidLatLng() validation prevents NaN errors, map loads without runtime errors, tiles render properly (no black map), surf spot markers display correctly, GPS Settings Guide modal opens when GPS fails.
critical_code_review_comments: isValidLatLng() function at MapPage.js lines 77-84 correctly validates coordinates before Leaflet operations
useUserLocation.js has NaN validation at lines 188-191 and 240-243 before setting location state
GPS Settings Guide (GPSSettingsGuide.js) properly shows when GPS fails with device-specific instructions
LocationPicker.js provides manual location selection as fallback
context_for_next_testing_agent: GPS/Map validation fixes are working correctly. The isValidLatLng() function prevents Invalid LatLng errors. When GPS fails or returns invalid data, the app gracefully shows the GPS Settings Guide modal instead of crashing. Map tiles load properly (no black map issue). Surf spot markers render with clustering.

map_page_loads_without_errors.details: Map page loads successfully, Leaflet container found, 40 tiles loaded

gps_button_no_nan_error.details: GPS button click handled gracefully - no Invalid LatLng or NaN errors detected

map_tiles_load_properly.details: 40 map tiles loaded, no black map issue

surf_spot_markers_render.details: 15 custom markers and 71 Leaflet markers visible with clustering

user_location_marker.details: User location marker code validates coordinates with isValidLatLng() before rendering

gps_settings_guide_modal.details: GPS Settings Guide modal opens when GPS fails, shows 'Fix Your Location' with iOS/Android instructions

manual_location_picker.details: LocationPicker.js component available as fallback for manual location selection

checks: isNaN check before setting location state

relevant_errors: [MAP] GPS error: {code: 2, message: Could not determine location} - Expected in test environment without GPS
